document_metadata.title: Complete Meridian FedRAMP 20x Phase One SRTM - All Worksheets

goals_and_objectives.description: Make it simple to automate the application and validation of FedRAMP security requirements.
80%+ of requirements will have automated validation without the need to write a single word about how it works, compared to 100% of current controls requiring narrative explanations
Technical controls will make sense and match standard configuration choices
Simplify the compliance process through automation and standardization
Reduce manual documentation burden on cloud service providers
Enable continuous compliance monitoring and validation
Improve 3PAO assessment efficiency through automated evidence collection
Establish machine-readable compliance validation framework
Create reusable compliance automation patterns for cloud architectures
Demonstrate practical implementation of cloud-native security controls
Validate effectiveness of automated security assessment approaches
Reduce time-to-authorization through streamlined assessment processes
Prove scalability of automated compliance validation across multiple cloud systems
Establish foundation for continuous authorization model
Create industry best practices for cloud security automation
Enable real-time compliance status reporting and monitoring
Reduce compliance operational costs through automation
Improve accuracy and consistency of security assessments
Facilitate faster security control implementation and validation
Support FedRAMP modernization through innovative assessment approaches

definitions.definition: Specific, measurable security capabilities required for FedRAMP authorization
definitions.notes: Designed for automated validation and continuous monitoring

definitions.definition: Core components that must be included in FedRAMP assessment boundary
definitions.notes: Defines minimum requirements for system boundary definition

definitions.definition: System design utilizing cloud-specific services and capabilities
Machine-executable verification of security control implementation
definitions.notes: Reduces manual assessment effort and improves consistency

definitions.definition: Ongoing automated assessment of security control effectiveness
definitions.notes: Supports real-time compliance status and risk management

definitions.definition: Infrastructure components that cannot be modified after deployment
definitions.notes: Deployed through infrastructure-as-code with version control

definitions.definition: Security model requiring verification for every access request
definitions.notes: Assumes no implicit trust based on network location

system_information.Authorization Boundary: Meridian LMS application and supporting AWS infrastructure
system_information.System Description: Cloud-based learning management system for educational institutions
system_information.Data Types: Educational content, user profiles, assessment data, institutional records

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Cloud service providers MUST apply ALL Key Security Indicators to ALL aspects of their cloud service offering that are within the FedRAMP Minimum Assessment Scope.
All parties SHOULD follow FedRAMP's best practices and technical assistance on assessing Key Security Indicators where applicable.
(INTERIM RULE) All parties SHOULD continuously monitor and review materials in the FedRAMP 20x Phase One (20xP1) pilot requirements and the 20x Community Working Group.
fedramp_rules.notes: Temporary rule for Phase One pilot program

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Cloud service providers MUST include all components within the Minimum Assessment Scope in their FedRAMP authorization boundary.
fedramp_rules.notes: Ensures comprehensive coverage of all critical components

fedramp_rules.rule_text: All MAS components must be properly documented and assessed by the 3PAO.
fedramp_rules.notes: Requires formal 3PAO validation of all MAS components

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Automated validation tools must be properly configured and validated before use in assessments.
fedramp_rules.notes: Ensures reliability of automated assessment results

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Automated validation results must be reviewed and verified by qualified assessment personnel.
fedramp_rules.notes: Maintains quality assurance in automated assessments

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Pilot participants must provide feedback on FedRAMP 20x processes and requirements.
Pilot systems must demonstrate practical implementation of automated validation approaches.
fedramp_rules.notes: Proves viability of automated assessment methods

fedramp_rules.rule_text: All evidence must be properly collected, stored, and made available for 3PAO review.
Automated evidence collection must maintain integrity and non-repudiation.
fedramp_rules.notes: Critical for audit and compliance purposes

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Systems must implement continuous monitoring capabilities for ongoing compliance validation.
fedramp_rules.notes: Foundation for future continuous authorization model

fedramp_rules.rule_text: Continuous monitoring results must be regularly reviewed and acted upon.
Pilot participants should explore innovative approaches to cloud security assessment.
All parties must actively participate in FedRAMP 20x community collaboration and knowledge sharing.
fedramp_rules.notes: Essential for collective advancement of cloud security practices

ksi_summary_complete.name: Cloud service providers MUST apply ALL Key Security Indicators
FedRAMP Best Practices and technical guidance
Community Working Group participation and monitoring
Configure ALL information resources to limit inbound and outbound traffic
Design systems to minimize attack surface and lateral movement
Use logical networking to enforce traffic flow controls
Use immutable infrastructure with defined functionality
Implement container security and orchestration controls
Ensure secure API design and implementation
Implement cloud-native security monitoring and alerting
Establish and maintain secure configuration baselines
Implement automated configuration compliance monitoring
Ensure secure service configuration and hardening
Implement vulnerability scanning and remediation
Establish secure development and deployment pipelines
Implement secure backup and recovery procedures
Ensure secure patch management processes
Implement comprehensive identity and access management
Enforce multi-factor authentication for privileged access
Implement role-based access controls and least privilege
Establish automated access review and certification
Implement session management and timeout controls
Ensure secure credential storage and management
Implement comprehensive logging and monitoring
Establish security event correlation and analysis
Implement automated alerting and notification systems
Ensure log integrity and protection
Implement performance monitoring and capacity management
Establish log retention and archival policies
Implement configuration management database
Establish automated configuration tracking and change detection
Implement change management and approval processes
Establish configuration baseline management
Implement configuration compliance reporting and remediation
Implement data classification and handling procedures
Establish privacy impact assessment processes
Implement data minimization and retention controls
Establish consent management and user rights procedures
Implement data breach notification procedures
Establish cross-border data transfer controls
Implement privacy monitoring and compliance reporting
Establish third-party risk assessment and management
Implement supply chain security controls
Establish vendor security monitoring and oversight
Implement third-party data sharing agreements and controls
Implement comprehensive encryption for data at rest
Ensure encryption for data in transit
Implement high availability and fault tolerance
Establish disaster recovery and business continuity
Implement performance monitoring and optimization
Establish capacity planning and scalability controls
Establish incident response procedures and capabilities
Implement security incident detection and analysis
Establish incident communication and reporting

submission_requirements.instructions: Complete 3PAO assessment report with findings and recommendations
Comprehensive system security documentation
Remediation plan for any identified deficiencies
Ongoing security monitoring and assessment procedures
Procedures for detecting, responding to, and recovering from incidents
Procedures for managing system configuration changes
Current vulnerability scan results and remediation status
Independent penetration testing results and remediation
Comprehensive risk assessment and mitigation strategies
Assessment of privacy implications and controls
Assessment of third-party and supply chain risks
Agreements for any system interconnections
All required FedRAMP templates and worksheets
Evidence of security control implementation
Results from automated security validation tools
System architecture and network topology documentation
Documentation of data flows and processing
Documentation of encryption methods and key management
Complete access control documentation and role definitions
Audit logging configuration and retention procedures
Data backup and system recovery documentation
Comprehensive disaster recovery and business continuity plan
Security training programs and awareness materials
Third-party vendor assessment and management procedures
Privacy control implementation and procedures
Physical security controls and procedures (for applicable components)

ksi_si_details.name: Traffic Controls
ksi_si_details.description: Configure ALL information resources to limit inbound and outbound traffic
ksi_si_details.test_method: Automated AWS CLI validation with manual verification

fedramp_rev5_information: FedRAMP Rev 5: Effective Monday, June 30, 2025 for LIMITED PILOT ADOPTION in ALL FedRAMP Rev5 authorizations.
This pilot system operates under FedRAMP 20x Phase One requirements with transition planning for Rev 5 compatibility.

assessment_overview.title: FedRAMP 20x Assessment Overview
assessment_overview.scope: All KSI, and All MAS Requirements

findings_and_recommendations.description: Multi-factor authentication implementation in progress for administrative accounts
findings_and_recommendations.recommendation: Complete MFA rollout for all privileged accounts by end of assessment period

findings_and_recommendations.description: Opportunity to automate additional privacy controls validation
findings_and_recommendations.recommendation: Explore automation opportunities for privacy compliance monitoring

findings_and_recommendations.description: Incident response procedures documentation in development
findings_and_recommendations.recommendation: Finalize incident response procedures and conduct tabletop exercise

findings_and_recommendations.description: Excellent implementation of automated validation framework
findings_and_recommendations.recommendation: Consider sharing automation framework as industry best practice

findings_and_recommendations.description: Pioneering use of JSON-based SRTM for compliance automation
findings_and_recommendations.recommendation: Document lessons learned for FedRAMP community
